items.title: Introducing our Science Blog
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has made several major announcements in early 2026. Most notably, on April 7, 2026, they unveiled Claude Mythos Preview — a new general-purpose model that is strikingly capable at computer security tasks. Alongside this, Anthropic launched Project Glasswing, an initiative using Mythos Preview to help secure critical software infrastructure. Earlier in February 2026, Claude Opus 4.6 was released with notably improved ability to find high-severity vulnerabilities, and a new probe-based d

items.title: Latest Claude Updates (2026): Release Notes, New Features & Timeline | Appscribed
items.author: appscribed.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a major 2026 with several significant Claude model releases. On February 5, 2026, Anthropic officially launched Claude Opus 4.6 featuring a 1-million-token context window, stronger coding skills, improved financial analysis (topping the Finance Agent benchmark), and a 14.5-hour task completion time horizon — the longest of any AI model. Claude Sonnet 4.6 also launched as their most capable Sonnet model yet, with full upgrades across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning,

items.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's most significant recent announcement (April 7, 2026) is the debut of a powerful new AI model called 'Mythos' via Project Glasswing, a cybersecurity initiative. The model represents a 'step change' in capabilities but was intentionally withheld from public release due to its advanced hacking abilities — marking one of the first times an AI company has held back a model over societal risk. Instead, Anthropic is making it available exclusively to major cybersecurity firms to help build
